refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not mix multiple requirements or specify how to achieve this latency, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety compatibility criteria, such as p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation
Design a real-time data compression and prioritization mechanism to minimize transmission delays
Establish a distributed control architecture with local autonomy to reduce dependency on end-to-end communication
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication latency
Use a modular communication interface that can adapt to varying network conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and control system could cause coupling
Dependency on specific network conditions may limit flexibility
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol design, which logically isolates the latency requirement from other system functions, supporting independence. However, care must be taken to avoid embedding control logic within the communication layer to maintain decoupling.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not impose additional constraints that increase latency
Predictive control or local autonomy is not required to meet latency under all conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive or predictive control strategies as separate DPs if network variability or safety policies demand latency compensation beyond communication protocol optimization.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for enabling remote control and communication between operator and vehicle

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for vehicle status and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and instructions from operator to vehicle

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting communication protocols

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion of communication protocol logic.
Vehicle module focuses on physical and embedded systems; communication protocol for teleoperation is only partially contained and would diffuse into other vehicle subsystems.
Highly cohesive for teleoperation communication protocol; low diffusion as protocol stack is central to teleoperation functionality.
Safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; communication protocol stack is peripheral and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if low-level network hardware interfaces are tightly coupled.
Changes in network technology might require coordination with NGHE Solution module if it manages broader network infrastructure.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If diffusion with Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules is problematic, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate protocol logic from hardware interface management, enabling clearer modular boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation, which is necessary to achieve the required end-to-end latency compatible with safe remote operation (p95 ≤ 150 ms). This mapping is clear and focused on the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if low-level network hardware interfaces are tightly coupled.
Changes in network technology might require coordination with NGHE Solution module if it manages broader network infrastructure.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating it is a highly cohesive and appropriate boundary for implementing the communication protocol stack. Other modules show higher diffusion risks and lower containment, supporting the choice of Teleoperation as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the end-to-end communication latency within the Teleoperation module under agreed network conditions, ensuring p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces defined for telemetry, tasking, and network link, supporting clear ownership and modularity.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly separate protocol logic from hardware interface management to reduce diffusion risks with the Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
Establish clear interface contracts and coordination mechanisms between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to manage low-level network hardware dependencies.
Monitor changes in network technology and update module boundaries or responsibilities accordingly to maintain modular clarity and minimize diffusion.

decision.justification: Although all gates passed indicating a clear and appropriate mapping from FR to DP to IM, the recommended revisions highlight important refinements needed to reduce diffusion risks and improve modular clarity, especially given the high safety criticality. These refinements involve separating protocol logic from hardware interface management and establishing clear interface contracts with related modules, which are critical to maintain safety and modular integrity. Therefore, revising the current level before proceeding further is prudent to ensure robustness and safety compliance.

revise.why: To explicitly separate protocol logic from hardware interface management and to establish clear interface contracts and coordination mechanisms with related modules, reducing diffusion risks and improving modular clarity in a safety-critical context.

assumptions: The current DP adequately addresses the FR but can be refined for better modular separation.
The Teleoperation IM boundary is appropriate but requires clearer interface definitions to manage dependencies.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion with Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules if hardware interfaces and network management are not clearly separated.
Changes in network technology may impact module boundaries and responsibilities.
evidence_needed: Verification results of latency measurements within the Teleoperation module under defined network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and coordination mechanisms between Teleoperation and related modules.
